Full text truncated. Paper focuses on silicone (not EVA/PEI) photodegradation of adhesion - tangentially relevant to general adhesion-degradation framework."}, {"source_url": "https://www.renewsysworld.com/_files/ugd/84a06c_290bda798b6949ab879ead6b6b23f93b.pdf?index=true", "source_title": "Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through compositional change in PV encapsulant poly(ethylene-co-vinyl acetate)", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.68, "overall_relevance": 0.75, "year": 2020, "authors": [], "venue": "RenewSys", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "PEVA films with 18, 24, 33, and 40% VA content were fabricated, cured at 150 degrees C under vacuum, and subjected to DH aging at 85 degrees C / 85% RH per IEC 61215 for 1000 and 2000 hours.", "direct_quote": "PEVA films containing 18, 24, 33, and 40% VA content with the necessary additives have been fabricated and cured at 150 degrees C under vacuum and subsequently subjected to accelerated damp-heat (85 degrees C and 85% RH) aging according to IEC 61215 standard for 1000 and 2000 h", "fact_category": "methodology", "relevance_score": 0.82, "confidence": 0.9, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["PEVA", "VA content", "IEC 61215", "85 degrees C", "85% RH"]}, {"statement": "Tensile strength, thermal stability, and crosslinking degree were greater for PEVA with lower VA content (18% and 24%) and diminished for higher VA content (33% and 40%) after DH aging.", "direct_quote": "Tensile strength, thermal stability and degree of crosslinking have been found to be greater for PEVA containing lower VA content (18 and 24%) and diminished for PEVA with higher VA content (33 and 40%) due to DH aging", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.88, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["PEVA", "tensile strength", "crosslinking", "VA content"]}, {"statement": "PEVA with 18% VA was discouraged as encapsulant due to inadequate transmittance and high stiffness after 2000 hours of DH aging.", "direct_quote": "inadequate % transmittance and relatively high stiffness after 2000 h of DH aging discourages PEVA18 as an encapsulant", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.78, "confidence": 0.85, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["PEVA18", "transmittance", "stiffness"]}, {"statement": "PEVAs with 33% and 40% VA degraded significantly after 2000 hours DH aging with significant loss in transmittance and mechanical integrity.", "direct_quote": "PEVAs with higher VA content (33 and 40%) have been degraded significantly after 2000 h of DH aging resulting in significant loss in transmittance and mechanical integrity", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.85, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["PEVA33", "PEVA40", "DH aging", "2000 h"]}, {"statement": "Results encourage the PV industry to reduce VA content in PEVA to 24% for better encapsulation response against damp heat aging.", "direct_quote": "results of this investigation encourage the PV industry to reduce the VA content in PEVA down to 24% for better encapsulation response against DH aging", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.8, "confidence": 0.85, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["PEVA", "24% VA", "DH aging"]}], "evidence_summary": "RenewSys study on VA content modulation in PEVA for DH resistance. Lower VA (24%) retains tensile/crosslinking better after IEC 61215 DH2000. Standard 33-40% VA degrades significantly. Recommends reducing from 28-33% to 24% for improved damp heat performance."}]}
